sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to fall away, break away from any one, followed by בְּ 2Ki 1:1 3:5 , 2Ki 3:7 (properly to break a covenant entered into with him; just like the Germ. mit jem. brechen, to break with any one. Compare Arab. فسق to fail from; compare Aram. ܦܣܰܩ, פְּסַק to break); מִתַּחַת 2Ki 8:20, 22 2Ki 8:22. Specially to turn away from God. Followed by בַּיְהֹוָה Isa 1:2 Jer 2:29 3:13 Hos 7:13. Hence-

(2) to sin, to transgress, Pro 28:21 followed by עַל (against) Hos 8:1. Part. פֹּשְׁעִים sinners (those who turn aside from God), Isa 1:28 46:8 and frequently.

Niphal, recipr. of No. 1. Pro 18:19, אָח נִפְשָׁע “brethren discordant,” among themselves (die mit einander brechen). Hence-
